title: IREN Q1 FY26 Results Summary
text: IREN reported record quarterly results for the period ending September 30, 2025. Total revenue was $240.3 million, up 355% year-over-year. Net income was $384.6 million versus a $51.7 million loss in Q1 FY25. Adjusted EBITDA was $91.7 million, representing a 3,568% increase. The company noted that net income figures include unrealized gains from prepaid forwards and capped calls tied to convertible notes. IREN secured a significant $9.7 billion contract with Microsoft featuring phased deployments at the Childress facility through 2026, with $1.9 billion expected annual revenue contribution and 20% customer prepayment structure. The company targets $3.4 billion in AI Cloud annualized run-rate revenue by the end of 2026 with expansion to 140,000 GPUs. As of October 31, 2025, IREN held $1.8 billion in cash and equivalents, bolstered by $1.0 billion in zero-coupon convertible notes issued October 14, 2025 and $400 million total GPU financing secured. Infrastructure development continues across three major sites: British Columbia with GPU transition from ASICs ongoing, Childress with liquid-cooled data center construction accelerating for Microsoft, and Sweetwater Hub with substation energization targeted for April 2026.
